prevalence.trend: stable; higher in women and older adults

incidence.source: Primary care and GI cohort syntheses

burden.dalyPerCapita: low mortality; significant QOL and healthcare utilization impact

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Rome IV functional constipation criteria; exclude secondary causes and alarm features

publicHealthSystem.controlRate: High with diet and evidence-based laxative regimens

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Primary care diagnosis with access to GI evaluation when needed
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Dietary fiber and fluids, PEG/lactulose, stimulant laxatives short-term; evaluate secondary causes
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Adherence and long-term management in older adults
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Wide access to effective laxatives and primary care
